generated_oml:
namespace base = "http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
namespace DTDFVocab = "http://www.example.com/DTDFVocab"
instance RealTimeVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time display of vehicle state and object tracks"
]
instance DataSaving : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Save sensor and processing data to SQL database",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance ReportGeneration : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Generate reports from aggregated data"
]
instance LeadVehicleDetection : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of lead vehicle position and speed"
]
instance SpeedComputation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Compute following speed based on distance to lead vehicle"
]
instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Send commands to actuators for speed control",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance EnableDisableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable or disable ACC functionality",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance SetDesiredSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set target speed for ACC",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance EmergencyBrakingCommand : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issue emergency braking command",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data, projected driving path, detected object tracks, data saving, and report generation",
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualization, DataSaving, ReportGeneration
]
instance ACCComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cloud-based offloading of ACC logic for lead vehicle detection and speed computation",
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetection, SpeedComputation, ActuationCommands
]
instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operator control for enabling/disabling ACC, setting desired speed, and emergency braking",
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableDisableACC, SetDesiredSpeed, EmergencyBrakingCommand
]
instance NVIDIAJetpack50 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 software development kit for low-latency communication and data collection",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ACCComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]
instance ROS2 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 middleware for communication and component integration",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ACCComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]
instance Python38 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 application modules for data processing",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ACCComputationOffloadingService
]
instance CPlusPlus : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ application modules for high-performance processing",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ACCComputationOffloadingService
]
instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables ACCComputationOffloadingService
]
instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for structured data storage and reporting",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables point cloud clustering and tracking analytics",

]
instance ACCEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables Adaptive Cruise Control logic",
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCComputationOffloadingService
]

    base:desc "Enables data storage and retrieval",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [

    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoLiDARPointCloud
]

    DTDFVocab:producedData IntelRealSenseDepthData
]

]
instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures steering angle of the vehicle",

    base:desc "Kalman filter-based object state estimation for point cloud clustering and tracking",

]
instance IntelRealSenseDepthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [

]

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands for motor and steering actuation are generated by the ACC Controlling FE and Control Panel User Interface FE, serialized via the Data Translation component, and transmitted over Wi-Fi using UDP to the Actuation FE for physical actuation. Specific command formats, validation mechanisms, feedback systems, and conflict resolution are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system enforces 100 ms clock synchronization accuracy for computation offloading and device coordination, a requirement dictated by hardware limitations despite industry standards for safety-critical vehicular applications mandating stricter synchronization (sub-100 ms)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The architecture follows ISO 23247 RA with entity-based organization, implementing each Functional Entity (FE) as individual ROS2 packages. The system comprises three entities deployed on different hardware: User Entity (3D Visualization and Control Panel), Digital Twin Entity (data management and control), and Physical Twin Entity (data pre-processing, actuation, device communication, and automotive functions like Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between software components occurs via ROS2, while inter-entity communication (physical to digital and vice versa) uses Wi-Fi with UDP in the transport layer. No details on network topology, bandwidth, latency requirements, reliability mechanisms, or security protocols are provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to ISO 23247-2021 standard, which consists of four parts: Overview and general principles, Reference architecture, Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements, and Information exchange. The Reference architecture (Part 2) defines entity-based structure including Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity. For information exchange (Part 4), OPC Unified Architecture is utilized as a protocol for data collection and device control interoperability."
]
